summary: Exceptional audience recording with broadcast-quality sound, capturing a solid 1980-era Grateful Dead show.
review: The tape, captured by Jim Wise using Sennheiser 421 microphones and Dan Healy’s front‑of‑house mix, rivals soundboard fidelity while preserving a dynamic live ambience. The performance is a typical but strong 1980 set that sounds great thanks to the pristine recording quality.

ai_rating.rationale: Review praises both the recording (broadcast‑quality, audience tape) and the solid 1980‑era performance; weighted average yields a high rating despite only one detailed review.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Minimal crowd cheer, dynamic ambience, front‑of‑house mix by Dan Healy; Sennheiser 421s into Sony D5 provide crisp, broadcast‑quality sound that rivals a soundboard.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Standard 1980‑era set with familiar songs; solid pacing and energy throughout.
